teachingHint: Your child learned the /ar/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading longer, more advanced words with 'ar': ch-ar-t → chart, sh-ar-p → sharp. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: ch-ar-t → 'ch' and 'ar' are digraphs. Blend: ch-ar-t → chart
sh-ar-p → 'sh' and 'ar' are digraphs. Blend: sh-ar-p → sharp
s-p-ar-k → 'ar' is a digraph. Blend: sp-ar-k → spark
sh-ar-k → 'sh' and 'ar' are digraphs. Blend: sh-ar-k → shark

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Oh' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'h' is silent. We say 'oh'. Learn it by sight.

story: Oh! Look at the chart. A sharp shark swam past with a spark in its eye!

teachingHint: More practice with the /ar/ sound in longer words: m-ar-k-e-t → market, g-ar-d-e-n → garden. Help your child sound out each part carefully. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: m-ar-k-e-t → 'ar' is a digraph. Blend: m-ar-k-e-t → market
g-ar-d-e-n → 'ar' is a digraph. Blend: g-ar-d-e-n → garden
c-ar-p-e-t → 'ar' is a digraph. Blend: c-ar-p-e-t → carpet
h-ar-v-e-s-t → 'ar' is a digraph. Blend: h-ar-v-e-s-t → harvest

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Their' doesn't follow normal rules. It sounds like 'there'. Learn it by sight.

story: Their garden has a red carpet and a market stall. It is time for the harvest!

teachingHint: Your child learned the /or/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading more advanced words with 'or': s-p-or-t → sport, s-t-or-m → storm. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-p-or-t → 'or' is a digraph. Blend: sp-or-t → sport
s-t-or-m → 'or' is a digraph. Blend: st-or-m → storm
c-or-n-er → 'or' and 'er' are digraphs. Blend: c-or-n-er → corner
m-or-n-i-ng → 'or' and 'ng' are digraphs. Blend: m-or-n-i-ng → morning

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'People' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'eo' makes an /ee/ sound and the 'le' at the end is tricky. Learn it by sight.

story: People love sport! One morning a storm hit the corner of the pitch.

teachingHint: More practice with the /or/ sound in longer words: f-or-t-y → forty, or-d-er → order. Help your child spot the 'or' digraph in each word. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: f-or-t-y → 'or' is a digraph. Blend: f-or-t-y → forty
i-m-p-or-t-a-n-t → 'or' is a digraph. A long word — take it slowly! Blend: i-m-p-or-t-a-n-t → important
or-d-er → 'or' and 'er' are digraphs. Blend: or-d-er → order
s-p-or-t → 'or' is a digraph. Blend: sp-or-t → sport

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Mr' is short for 'Mister'. We say 'mister' but write 'Mr'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mr Fox called out forty important rules. "Keep them in order!" he said about sport.

teachingHint: Your child learned the /ur/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading more advanced words with 'ur': ch-ur-ch → church, p-ur-p-l-e → purple. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: ch-ur-ch → 'ch' is a digraph and 'ur' is a digraph. Blend: ch-ur-ch → church
p-ur-p-l-e → 'ur' is a digraph. Blend: p-ur-p-l-e → purple
t-ur-t-l-e → 'ur' is a digraph. Blend: t-ur-t-l-e → turtle
d-i-s-t-ur-b → 'ur' is a digraph. A long word — take it slowly! Blend: d-i-s-t-ur-b → disturb

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Mrs' is short for 'Missus'. We say 'missus' but write 'Mrs'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mrs Plum saw a purple turtle near the church. "Do not disturb it!" she said.

teachingHint: More practice with the /ur/ sound in longer words: r-e-t-ur-n → return, s-ur-p-r-i-s-e → surprise. Help your child spot the 'ur' digraph in each word. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: r-e-t-ur-n → 'ur' is a digraph. Blend: r-e-t-ur-n → return
s-ur-p-r-i-s-e → 'ur' is a digraph. A long word — take it slowly! Blend: s-ur-p-r-i-s-e → surprise
ch-ur-ch → 'ch' and 'ur' are digraphs. Blend: ch-ur-ch → church
t-ur-t-l-e → 'ur' is a digraph. Blend: t-ur-t-l-e → turtle

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Looked' has the /ed/ ending that sounds like /t/. The 'oo' makes the /u/ sound. Learn it by sight.

story: We looked for the turtle and got a surprise! It had come back. "It will return to the church," she said.

teachingHint: Your child learned the /er/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading more advanced words with 'er': n-e-v-er → never, r-i-v-er → river. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: n-e-v-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: n-e-v-er → never
r-i-v-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: r-i-v-er → river
w-i-n-t-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: w-i-n-t-er → winter
a-f-t-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: a-f-t-er → after

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Called' has a tricky /ed/ ending that sounds like /d/. The 'a' makes an /or/ sound. Learn it by sight.

story: After winter, we called out to the river. "It will never stop flowing!" we said.

teachingHint: More practice with the /er/ sound in longer words: t-i-g-er → tiger, c-l-e-v-er → clever. Help your child spot the 'er' digraph at the end of each word. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: o-v-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: o-v-er → over
t-i-g-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: t-i-g-er → tiger
c-o-v-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: c-o-v-er → cover
c-l-e-v-er → 'er' is a digraph. Blend: c-l-e-v-er → clever

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Asked' has a tricky /ed/ ending that sounds like /t/. Learn it by sight.

story: "Can a clever tiger jump over that cover?" asked the boy. The tiger was too clever to try!

teachingHint: Your child learned the /air/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading more advanced words with 'air': r-e-p-air → repair, u-n-f-air → unfair. The 'air' is a trigraph — three letters making one sound. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: r-e-p-air → 'air' is a trigraph (three letters, one sound). Blend: r-e-p-air → repair
air-p-or-t → 'air' is a trigraph and 'or' is a digraph. Blend: air-p-or-t → airport
u-p-s-t-air-s → 'air' is a trigraph. A long word — take it slowly! Blend: u-p-st-air-s → upstairs
u-n-f-air → 'air' is a trigraph. Blend: u-n-f-air → unfair

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Could' has a silent 'l' and the 'ou' doesn't make its usual sound. We say /cud/. Learn it by sight.

story: "It is unfair!" she said. "I could not get upstairs at the airport to repair my bag!"

teachingHint: More practice with the /air/ trigraph in advanced words. This set revisits 'air' words to build fluency and confidence. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: u-n-f-air → 'air' is a trigraph. Blend: u-n-f-air → unfair
r-e-p-air → 'air' is a trigraph. Blend: r-e-p-air → repair
u-p-s-t-air-s → 'air' is a trigraph. Blend: u-p-st-air-s → upstairs
air-p-or-t → 'air' is a trigraph and 'or' is a digraph. Blend: air-p-or-t → airport

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'People' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'eo' makes an /ee/ sound and the 'le' at the end is tricky. Learn it by sight.

story: People said it was unfair. They had to go upstairs at the airport to repair the door.

teachingHint: Your child learned the /ear/ sound in Phase 3. Now they're reading more advanced words with 'ear': c-l-ear → clear, a-pp-ear → appear. The 'ear' is a trigraph — three letters making one sound. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-l-ear → 'ear' is a trigraph (three letters, one sound). Blend: c-l-ear → clear
a-p-p-ear → 'ear' is a trigraph. The two p's are sounded separately. Blend: a-p-p-ear → appear
n-ear-l-y → 'ear' is a trigraph. Blend: n-ear-l-y → nearly
c-l-ear → 'ear' is a trigraph. Blend: c-l-ear → clear. Can you read it quickly this time?

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Their' doesn't follow normal rules. It sounds like 'there'. Learn it by sight.

story: It was nearly clear outside. Their dog would appear from behind the tree any moment!
